body.title: Escalation becomes a POLICY object with a proxy-side default; retire escalation_allowed and harvest_mode flags
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-08: 'The inference proxy is not non-escalating. For a chat conversation it handles the escalation. But for coding, each different experiment may want different escalation behavior — a default path for easy queries, and a more complicated path specified within that domain.'

Escalation becomes DATA, not control flow scattered across BaseDomain.run() and device.py:
  EscalationPolicy = ladder (which rungs, in order) + bump rule (which SIGNAL advances) + terminal.
  DEFAULT (proxy-side; chat gets escalation free) | NoEscalation (proofs) | Harvest (fixed tier, the wall IS the signal) | domain-specific.

This COLLAPSES two ad-hoc flags into one object: RouteRequest.escalation_allowed is not an escalation flag at all (its only effect is whether resolve() fires the terminal alarm) — it is the degenerate policy NoEscalation. BaseDomain.harvest_mode is a bool gating the bump — it is the Harvest policy.

ANTI-DOUBLE-SPEND: every signal has exactly ONE observer (see the decision's signal-ownership table). Proxy observes call-boundary signals (no capable model, provider down, context overflow, cost cap). The DOMAIN observes what only it can see (loop hit a terminal without DONE; max turns; prose instead of tool calls). One ladder, two observers, disjoint signals, ONE terminal alarm.

GATED on T-inference-tier-ladder-real (nothing to walk) and T-inference-typed-no-path-result (the bump rule needs the type).

**Test plan:** red->green: the same request under DEFAULT vs NoEscalation vs Harvest yields different, asserted behavior; a single capability wall advances exactly ONE rung (no double-spend); exactly one terminal alarm fires.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Note [2026-07-08 22:08]:** RETARGETED by Amendment 2 of D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations. The DEFAULT escalation policy does NOT live in the proxy — it lives on the GENERAL DOMAIN, because 'the default IS the general domain; there is always a domain, and everybody else descends from that' (Akien). PROXY = REPORTER (routes, dispatches, returns a typed result, NEVER escalates). DOMAIN = DECIDER (owns the policy; the general domain owns the default; specializations inherit and override only what they earn). The anti-double-spend signal-ownership table DISSOLVES — there is only one escalator, so it reduces to reporter/decider: proxy reports a path | NoCapableModel | NoAvailableProvider; the domain decides capability->advance a rung (spend up) vs availability->retry same rung (no spend). The typed no-path result is therefore the proxy's ENTIRE contribution to escalation and is strictly MORE necessary (T-inference-typed-no-path-result). ONE terminal alarm, fired by the domain when its ladder runs out. escalation_allowed -> NoEscalation policy; harvest_mode -> Harvest policy; both still collapse into the policy object.
body.result: proof OK — tests/inference/test_escalation_policy.py::test_three_policies_yield_distinct_walk_behavior red=AssertionError green=passed @ 171e9d2f; escalation is DATA (EscalationPolicy: escalates+on_wall); harvest_mode→HARVEST_POLICY, escalation_allowed retired (behavior-neutral, separate commit 63c09e33); 508 inference tests green. Discovered+ticketed pre-existing test_inference_via_bus gap (T-inference-via-bus-tests-typed-no-path-gap).
